title: Molten Sweet Tunisian Lamb En-croute
description: A rich and aromatic Tunisian-inspired dish featuring tender lamb, sweet cranberries, and a crispy, buttery crust.

ingredients.name: 1 1/2 pounds boneless lamb shoulder or leg

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, onion, garlic, cranberries, honey, mustard, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the pie crust to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the pie crust to a 9x13-inch baking dish and trim the edges to fit.
Spoon the lamb mixture into the center of the pie crust, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the pie crust up over the lamb mixture, pressing gently to seal.

Bake the en-croute for 25 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) and continue baking for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the lamb is cooked through.
Remove the en-croute from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
